mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS grows steadily over the prior 3 months without interruption, it signals robust labor market expansion that supports household income and corporate revenues, resulting in positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns relative to periods with flat or declining payrolls.
evidence_refs: fact:US payroll employment trending upward into late 2019 expansion
failure_condition: Across a rolling 10-year sample, episodes where PAYEMS rises in each of the prior 3 months fail to show average 20-day SPX returns that are at least 0.5 standard deviations above the unconditional mean, or the Rank IC between 3-month PAYEMS momentum and 20-day returns remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 1% and either stable or falling over the prior 2 months, the low policy rate level lowers discount rates without yet signaling acute recession risk, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate cut to near 0.08% by late 2019
failure_condition: During all identified episodes since 1985 when FEDFUNDS is below 1% and non-increasing over 2 months, the average 60-day SPX return is not significantly above zero at the 5% level, or the share of negative 60-day returns exceeds 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS declines by more than 3% over the prior 2 months, it signals weakening consumer goods demand that leads investors to downgrade earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns relative to periods with flat or rising retail sales.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales show late-cycle softening risks despite still-positive GDP in 2019
failure_condition: Over a historical sample, in windows where 2-month RSAFS growth is below -3%, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly below the unconditional average at the 5% level, or at least 55% of triggered episodes have non-negative 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE of at least 0.4 percentage points over the prior 3 months signals a material deterioration in labor market conditions that raises recession risk and compresses risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns compared with periods of stable unemployment.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment remained relatively low in 2019 but past spikes preceded equity drawdowns
failure_condition: Across all post-1980 episodes where UNRATE rises by at least 0.4pp in 3 months, the proportion of negative 60-day SPX returns does not exceed 55%, or mean 60-day returns are not at least 0.5 standard deviations below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO declines for at least 3 consecutive months while UNRATE is rising over the same period, the joint signal of contracting industrial output and softening labor markets indicates an impending cyclical downturn that leads to negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls in each of the last 3 months AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, combined declines in production and rising unemployment align with equity selloffs
failure_condition: In historical samples since 1970, during episodes where INDPRO falls for 3 straight months and UNRATE rises over 3 months, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative at the 5% level or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation is between 1% and 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 2%, the combination of moderate inflation and low policy rates supports high equity valuations without immediate policy tightening risk, leading to positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: Annual inflation from CPIAUCSL between 1% and 3% AND FEDFUNDS < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:Late 2010s featured moderate inflation and low rates with strong equity performance
failure_condition: Across all months where CPI inflation is between 1% and 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 2%, the mean 60-day SPX return is not significantly above zero at the 5% level or positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in GDPC1 over the prior year, defined as at least four consecutive positive quarterly changes, signals a durable expansion in real output that underpins earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns on average during such expansion phases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 has positive quarter-on-quarter growth for the last 4 quarters
evidence_refs: fact:US real GDP in 2018-2019 remained on an expansionary path
failure_condition: Over post-1960 data, during periods where GDPC1 has positive growth in four consecutive quarters, the average 20-day SPX return is not statistically higher than the unconditional mean at the 5% level or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of such quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST drops by more than 10% over the prior 6 months while overall GDPC1 growth over the same period remains positive, it signals a housing-specific slowdown within a still-growing economy that tightens financial conditions and sentiment disproportionately, leading to negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns as investors rapidly reprice housing-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: 6-month percent change in HOUST < -10% AND 6-month change in GDPC1 > 0
evidence_refs: fact:Housing slowdowns inside expansions have coincided with sharp equity pullbacks in prior cycles
regime_hint: late-cycle housing slowdown within ongoing expansion
failure_condition: In historical episodes where HOUST falls more than 10% over 6 months while GDPC1 remains positive over 6 months, the average 5-day SPX return is not significantly negative at the 5% level or negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.
